sourceMovie.title: Tomorrowland
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Tomorrowland (2015) stuck because it commits to its adventure premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Brad Bird's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The adventure and family blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Honey, I Shrunk the Kids
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Tomorrowland clicked for you, Honey, I Shrunk the Kids (1989) is a strong next stop: both trade in adventure and family, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Paycheck
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Tomorrowland clicked for you, Paycheck (2003) is a strong next stop: both trade in adventure and mystery, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Crater
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Tomorrowland clicked for you, Crater (2023) is a strong next stop: both trade in adventure and family, with earned warmth without mush. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Back to the Future

faq.question: What should I watch after Tomorrowland?
faq.answer: Try Honey, I Shrunk the Kids, Paycheck, A Grand Day Out—each shares DNA with Tomorrowland while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Tomorrowland?
faq.answer: Tomorrowland (2015) is best described as Adventure, Family, Mystery, Science Fiction. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Tomorrowland worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.3/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this adventure lane.
